mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls reduces aggregate wage income and expected corporate revenues, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Payrolls fell sharply through Q4 2008 and January 2009
failure_condition: Positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS declines month-over-month during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate at or below 0.25% signals the zero lower bound and maximum monetary accommodation, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is at or below 0.25% during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and rises in the unemployment rate generate a consumption-income drag that amplifies negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales and labor market both deteriorated in late 2008
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between the joint RSAFS-UNRATE signal and 20-day S&P 500 returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: After three consecutive monthly declines, a positive month-over-month change in industrial production signals inventory restocking and operating leverage recovery, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises month-over-month following three consecutive declines
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production collapsed in Q4 2008 and was approaching a turning point by early 2009
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO turns positive after three consecutive declines during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 7% signals severe slack in the labor market that crushes consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50% of months when UNRATE exceeds 7.0% during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales produce a reinforcing aggregate demand contraction that exceeds single-series effects, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Both payrolls and retail sales fell sharply in late 2008
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-RSAFS signal fails to produce a walk-forward Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or negative 5-day returns occur less than 55% of the time when both series decline.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year consumer price inflation above 3% erodes real corporate earnings and triggers tighter monetary policy expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50% of quarters when CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 3% during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 1% combined with housing starts below 700 thousand units signals a coordinated policy and sectoral distress floor that compresses equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% and HOUST at 625k as of late 2008
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS-HOUST signal produces negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances during walk-forward testing.
